parts.thinking:
The user is asking about what agent framework Crush uses. From reading the code, I can see:

1. **Crush uses NO existing agent framework** (no langgraph, openhands, openclaw, etc.)
2. It uses `charm.land/fantasy` as the LLM provider abstraction layer (not an agent framework - it's just a library that handles protocol differences between Anthropic, OpenAI, Gemini, etc.)
3. The core agent runtime is self-implemented in:
   - `internal/agent/agent.go` - `SessionAgent` struct (the main conversation loop)
   - `internal/agent/coordinator.go` - `Coordinator` struct (manages named agents like "coder", "task")
   - `internal/agent/tools/` - All built-in tools implemented from scratch
   - `internal/agent/hooked_tool.go` - Hook decorator pattern
   - `internal/agent/prompts.go` / `internal/agent/templates/` - System prompt management

The only external dependency that could be confused as a "framework" is `fantasy`, but it's just a provider abstraction layer - it handles HTTP calls to different LLM APIs, not agent orchestration. The agent loop, tool execution, message handling, session management, etc. are all custom.

parts.text:
Crush 没有使用任何现有的 Agent 框架（如 langgraph/openhands/openclaw）。

它的核心 Agent Runtime 是**完全自实现的**：

| 组件 | 功能 | 文件 |
|------|------|------|
| `SessionAgent` | LLM 对话主循环（发消息→收工具调用→执行→循环） | `internal/agent/agent.go` |
| `Coordinator` | 管理多个命名 Agent（"coder"、"task"）的编排 | `internal/agent/coordinator.go` |
| 工具系统 | 全部手写：bash、edit、view、grep、glob 等 20+ 工具 | `internal/agent/tools/*.go` |
| Hook 装饰器 | 工具执行前/后的 hook 拦截 | `internal/agent/hooked_tool.go` |
| Prompt 模板 | Go template 渲染系统提示词 | `internal/agent/templates/` |

唯一的 LLM 相关依赖是 `charm.land/fantasy`（`go.mod:10`），但它 **不是 Agent 框架**——只是一个 provider 抽象层，统一 Anthropic/OpenAI/Gemini 等 API 的协议差异。Agent loop、tool orchestration、session 管理、token 控制全部是 Crush 自己实现的。

对于面试题的要求来说，Crush 是很好的参考——它的架构就是"不依赖任何 Agent 框架，自行实现核心 Runtime"的典范。
“核心 Agent Runtime”如何彻底理解

    76|type SessionAgentCall struct {
    77|	SessionID string
    78|	// RunID, when non-empty, is the caller-supplied correlator that
    79|	// gets echoed back on the notify.RunComplete event emitted for
    80|	// this turn. It is preserved when the call is enqueued behind a
    81|	// busy session so the queued turn's terminal event is still
    82|	// recognisable to the original caller. Callers that need a
    83|	// reliable completion contract (e.g. `crush run` against a
    84|	// session that may be busy) MUST set it; SessionID alone is
    85|	// ambiguous when concurrent turns share the same session.
    86|	RunID            string
    87|	Prompt           string
    88|	ProviderOptions  fantasy.ProviderOptions
    89|	Attachments      []message.Attachment
    90|	MaxOutputTokens  int64
    91|	Temperature      *float64
    92|	TopP             *float64
    93|	TopK             *int64
    94|	FrequencyPenalty *float64
    95|	PresencePenalty  *float64
    96|	NonInteractive   bool
    97|	// OnComplete, when non-nil, replaces the default RunComplete
    98|	// publish path: the inner Run hands the terminal payload to this
    99|	// callback instead of emitting it on the RunComplete broker. The
   100|	// coordinator uses this hook to coalesce the unauthorized →
   101|	// re-auth → retry chain into a single user-visible terminal
   102|	// event, so non-interactive clients (e.g. `crush run`) don't
   103|	// exit on a stale failed-attempt RunComplete before the
   104|	// successful retry. It is intentionally stripped when queueing
   105|	// a busy-session call (see Run): the originating
   106|	// coordinator.Run has long returned by the time the queued
   107|	// recursion drains, so falling back to the default broker
   108|	// publish keeps the event visible to subscribers.
   109|	OnComplete func(notify.RunComplete)
   110|	// Accepted, when non-nil, is the accept reservation taken by
   111|	// BeginAccepted before the call was dispatched onto a goroutine
   112|	// (the client/server fire-and-forget path). Run consumes it under
   113|	// dispatchMu[SessionID] once the accepted -> (cancel-on-entry |
   114|	// queued | active) transition has been chosen. When nil
   115|	// (in-process / local callers like AppWorkspace), behavior is
   116|	// unchanged and no accept tracking applies.
   117|	Accepted *AcceptedRun
   118|	// acceptSeq carries the accept sequence of the handle that produced
   119|	// this call after it has been enqueued and its Accepted handle
   120|	// stripped. The queue-drain paths compare it against a session's
   121|	// cancel mark so a follow-up queued before a cancel is dropped while
   122|	// one queued after the cancel survives. 0 means untracked (an
   123|	// in-process enqueue with no accept reservation), which the drain
   124|	// paths treat as covered by any present mark, preserving the
   125|	// pre-sequence behavior.
   126|	acceptSeq uint64
   127|	// OnAuthRefresh, when non-nil, is called by fantasy when a stream
   128|	// fails with an authentication error (HTTP 401). The callback should
   129|	// refresh credentials and return nil on success, in which case
   130|	// fantasy retries the stream transparently. Returning an error
   131|	// surfaces the original auth error without retry.
   132|	OnAuthRefresh func(ctx context.Context, err *fantasy.ProviderError) error
   133|}

    15|
    16|// hookedTool wraps a fantasy.AgentTool to run PreToolUse hooks before
    17|// delegating to the inner tool.
    18|type hookedTool struct {
    19|	inner  fantasy.AgentTool
    20|	runner *hooks.Runner
    21|}
    22|
    23|func newHookedTool(inner fantasy.AgentTool, runner *hooks.Runner) *hookedTool {
    24|	return &hookedTool{inner: inner, runner: runner}
    25|}
    26|
    27|// wrapToolsWithHooks returns a tool slice with each entry wrapped in a
    28|// hookedTool. Returns the original slice unchanged when runner is nil or
    29|// when isSubAgent is true — sub-agents never fire hooks, the top-level
    30|// invocation of the sub-agent tool itself is wrapped on the caller's side.
    31|func wrapToolsWithHooks(tools []fantasy.AgentTool, runner *hooks.Runner, isSubAgent bool) []fantasy.AgentTool {
    32|	if runner == nil || isSubAgent {
    33|		return tools
    34|	}
    35|	out := make([]fantasy.AgentTool, len(tools))
    36|	for i, tool := range tools {
    37|		out[i] = newHookedTool(tool, runner)
    38|	}
    39|	return out
    40|}
    41|
    42|func (h *hookedTool) Info() fantasy.ToolInfo {
    43|	return h.inner.Info()
    44|}
    45|
    46|func (h *hookedTool) ProviderOptions() fantasy.ProviderOptions {
    47|	return h.inner.ProviderOptions()
    48|}
    49|
    50|func (h *hookedTool) SetProviderOptions(opts fantasy.ProviderOptions) {
    51|	h.inner.SetProviderOptions(opts)
    52|}
    53|
    54|func (h *hookedTool) Run(ctx context.Context, call fantasy.ToolCall) (fantasy.ToolResponse, error) {
    55|	sessionID := tools.GetSessionFromContext(ctx)
    56|	result, err := h.runner.Run(ctx, hooks.EventPreToolUse, sessionID, call.Name, call.Input)
    57|	if err != nil {
    58|		slog.Warn("Hook execution error, proceeding with tool call",
    59|			"tool", call.Name, "error", err)
    60|	}
    61|
    62|	if result.Decision == hooks.DecisionDeny || result.Halt {
    63|		reason := fmt.Sprintf("Tool call blocked by hook. Reason: %s", result.Reason)
    64|		if result.Halt {
    65|			reason = fmt.Sprintf("Turn halted by hook. Reason: %s", result.Reason)
    66|		}
    67|		resp := fantasy.NewTextErrorResponse(reason)
    68|		// Halt ends the whole turn; a plain deny only blocks this tool
    69|		// call so the model can see the error and try something else.
    70|		resp.StopTurn = result.Halt
    71|		resp.Metadata = hookMetadataJSON(result)
    72|		return resp, nil
    73|	}
    74|
    75|	if result.UpdatedInput != "" {
    76|		call.Input = result.UpdatedInput
    77|	}
    78|
    79|	// An explicit allow from a hook pre-approves the permission prompt for
    80|	// this tool call. Deny is already handled above; silence falls through
    81|	// to the normal permission flow.
    82|	if result.Decision == hooks.DecisionAllow {
    83|		ctx = permission.WithHookApproval(ctx, call.ID)
    84|	}
    85|
    86|	resp, err := h.inner.Run(ctx, call)
    87|	if err != nil {
    88|		return resp, err
    89|	}
    90|
    91|	if result.Context != "" {
    92|		if resp.Content != "" {
    93|			resp.Content += "\n"
    94|		}
    95|		resp.Content += result.Context
    96|	}
    97|
    98|	resp.Metadata = mergeHookMetadata(resp.Metadata, result)
    99|	return resp, nil
   100|}
   101|
   102|// buildHookMetadata creates a HookMetadata from an AggregateResult.
   103|func buildHookMetadata(result hooks.AggregateResult) hooks.HookMetadata {
   104|	return hooks.HookMetadata{
   105|		HookCount:    result.HookCount,
   106|		Decision:     result.Decision.String(),
   107|		Halt:         result.Halt,
   108|		Reason:       result.Reason,
   109|		InputRewrite: result.UpdatedInput != "",
   110|		Hooks:        result.Hooks,
   111|	}
   112|}
   113|
   114|// hookMetadataJSON builds a JSON string containing only the hook metadata.
   115|func hookMetadataJSON(result hooks.AggregateResult) string {
   116|	meta := buildHookMetadata(result)
   117|	data, err := json.Marshal(meta)
   118|	if err != nil {
   119|		return ""
   120|	}
   121|	return `{"hook":` + string(data) + `}`
   122|}
   123|
   124|// mergeHookMetadata injects hook metadata into existing tool metadata.
   125|func mergeHookMetadata(existing string, result hooks.AggregateResult) string {
   126|	if result.HookCount == 0 {
   127|		return existing
   128|	}
   129|	meta := buildHookMetadata(result)
   130|	data, err := json.Marshal(meta)
   131|	if err != nil {
   132|		return existing
   133|	}
   134|	if existing == "" {
   135|		existing = "{}"
   136|	}
   137|	merged, err := sjson.SetRaw(existing, "hook", string(data))
   138|	if err != nil {
   139|		return existing
   140|	}
   141|	return merged
   142|}

以 Crush 源码为例，"核心 Agent Runtime" 指的是以下**4 层自实现机制**：

---

### ① Agent Loop（`internal/agent/agent.go:557-1207`）

`sessionAgent.Run()` 是整个系统的**心脏**，它是一个完整的 `Stream → ToolCall → ToolResult → Stream` 循环：

```
用户输入 → agent.Stream() → LLM 流式返回
  → 如果是文本 → 展示给用户 → 结束
  → 如果是工具调用 → PrepareStep(注入最新 tools/queue)
    → OnToolCall(记录调用)
    → fantasy 内部执行工具 → OnToolResult(保存结果)
    → 循环回到 agent.Stream()（LLM 看到 tool result 后继续推理）
```

关键点：**这个循环不是任何框架提供的**，是自己手写的 `PrepareStep` + `OnToolCall` + `OnToolResult` + `StopWhen` 回调组合。

### ② 工具系统（`internal/agent/tools/*.go`）

每个工具（bash、edit、view、grep 等）都实现 `fantasy.AgentTool` 接口（仅 `Info()` + `Run()`），没有任何框架约束。工具的**权限检查、hook 拦截、MCP 工具注入**都是 Crush 自己的装饰器：

```
AgentTool
  → hookedTool (hook 拦截, agent.go:54-100)
    → permission check (在 tool Run 方法内部)
      → 真实执行
```

### ③ 会话与消息持久化（`internal/session/`, `internal/message/`）

自行管理 SQLite 中的 session 和 message，包括：
- 自动摘要（超过 context window 阈值时自动压缩）
- Run queue（同 session 多请求排队）
- Cancel 机制（`dispatchMu` + `cancelMark` 精确取消）

### ④ 协调器（`internal/agent/coordinator.go:220-322`）

`coordinator.run()` 是上层编排：
- 等待 MCP 初始化
- 刷新模型配置
- 处理 OAuth 刷新/重试链
- 合并 per-attempt 的 RunComplete 事件（避免 `crush run` 因首次失败退出）

---

**`fantasy` 扮演的角色**（`charm.land/fantasy`）：它只是 **LLM Provider 的 HTTP 客户端抽象**——把 Anthropic Messages API、OpenAI Chat Completions、Google Gemini API 等统一成 `Stream()` 调用。它不是 Agent 框架，就像 `database/sql` 不是 ORM 框架一样。

**总结**：Agent Runtime = 你自己写的 `while(true) { llm_call → tool_exec → save_state → ... }` 循环 + 工具注册/执行机制 + 会话管理 + 编排逻辑。Crush 这 4 层全部自己实现，没有一行 langgraph/openhands 的代码。
这个crush项目的”核心 Agent Runtime“代码多少？我打算直接复刻他作为我的笔试题目答案

## Crush 核心 Runtime 代码量

| 层级 | 文件 | 行数 |
|------|------|------|
| **Agent Loop** | `agent.go` | 2,275 |
| **协调编排** | `coordinator.go` | 1,586 |
| **小件** | hooked_tool / prompts / errors / event / runid / loop_detection / agent_tool / agentic_fetch_tool | 696 |
| **工具实现** | `tools/*.go`（bash, edit, view, write, ls, grep, glob, fetch 等 20+ 工具） | 8,499 |
| **提示词模板** | `templates/*` | 500 |
| **消息模型** | `message/*.go` | 1,896 |
| **会话管理** | `session/*.go` | 394 |
| **权限** | `permission/*.go` | 803 |
| **总计** | | **~16,600** |

---

**坦白说**：直接复刻 1.6 万行作为笔试题答案风险很大——面试官很可能看过 Crush（尤其 Charm 的项目知名度不低）。

真正笔试要体现的是**自己理解了这个架构后，用最精简的方式重新实现**。核心就 3 个文件就能讲清楚原理：

1. **`agent.go`（~200 行）** — LLM 调用 + tool loop
2. **`coordinator.go`（~100 行）** — 组装 prompt + 编排 agent
3. **`tool.go`（~50 行）** — tool 接口 + 注册

核心思想是：**`Stream → ToolCall → ToolResult → Stream` 这个循环是你自己写的**，不是框架给的。用 `fantasy` 这样的 provider 抽象库是可以接受的（就像用 `database/sql` 不算用 ORM 框架），关键是 agent orchestration 逻辑自己掌控。

Now I can clearly see the breakdown. Let me analyze what's really "core runtime" vs "extra features" in Crush's 16k lines.

**agent.go (2,275 lines, 45+ functions):**

Core (the absolute minimum for an agent loop):
- `Run` — the main loop (~600 lines)
- `createUserMessage` — save user input
- `getSessionMessages` — load history
- `preparePrompt` — build messages for LLM
- `ValidateCall` — validate input

Non-core (product features):
- `Summarize` — auto-context-compression
- `GenerateTitle` — auto-title generation
- `Cancel`/`CancelAll` — cancellation
- `ClearQueue`/`QueuedPrompts` — queue management
- `SetModels`/`SetTools`/`SetSystemPrompt` — dynamic reconfiguration
- `beginAccepted`/`endAccepted`/`sessionMu`/`enqueueCall`/`drainQueueForStep` — queue+dispatch system
- `persistCanceledTurn`/`publishCanceledQueueDrops` — graceful cancellation
- `convertToToolResult` — type conversion
- `workaroundProviderMediaLimitations` — provider compatibility
- `sanitizeToolInput` — input sanitation
- `openrouterCost`/`extractHyperCredits` — cost tracking
- `updateSessionUsage`/`updateSessionTokenCounters` — usage stats
- `summaryCompletionTokens` — summarization
- `getCacheControlOptions` — Anthropic caching
- `sessionHeaders` — networking
- `filterFileParts`/`filterOrphanedToolResults` — message cleanup
- `providerRetryLogFields` — logging

**coordinator.go (1,586 lines, 40+ functions):**

Core:
- `run()` — entry point that calls agent.Run

Non-core:
- Provider building: `buildAnthropicProvider`, `buildOpenaiProvider`, `buildOpenrouterProvider`, `buildVercelProvider`, `buildOpenaiCompatProvider`, `buildAzureProvider`, `buildBedrockProvider`, `buildGoogleProvider`, `buildGoogleVertexProvider`, `buildProvider` — 10 functions for different LLM providers
- `buildAgent`/`buildTools`/`buildAgentModels` — agent setup
- `refreshTokenIfExpired`/`runWithUnauthorizedRetry`/`retryAfterUnauthorized` — OAuth token refresh
- `runSubAgent` — sub-agent support
- `discoverSkills`/`logTurnSkillUsage` — skills

**tools/*.go (8,499 lines):**

Many of these are complex tools that a basic exam wouldn't need:
- `bash.go` — shell execution (~300+ lines)
- `edit.go`/`multiedit.go` — file editing
- `fetch.go`/`web_fetch.go`/`web_search.go` — web access
- `fetch_helpers.go`/`fetch_types.go` — HTTP client
- `sourcegraph.go` — code search
- `todos.go` — task management
- `question.go` — user interaction
- `mcp/` — MCP protocol integration
- `safe.go` — safety checks
- `rg.go` — ripgrep integration

A basic tool system only needs: `bash`, `edit`, `view`, `write`, `ls`, `grep`, `glob` (~7 tools, ~500-1000 lines total)

**message/*.go (1,896 lines):**

This is the message model with all content types, database serialization, streaming updates, etc. A basic version could be ~200 lines.

**session/*.go (394 lines) + permission/*.go (803 lines):**

Session persistence and permission system. For an exam, sessions could be in-memory (~100 lines), permissions could be a simple allow/deny check.

So the actual "core runtime" that an interview question would test is MUCH smaller - maybe 500-1000 lines total.

## 核心 Runtime（~800 行）

```
agent.go:
  Run()                   ~200行    Stream→ToolCall→Result→Stream 循环
  createUserMessage()      ~20行    保存用户输入
  getSessionMessages()     ~30行    加载历史
```

## 正确的做法

不是"从 Crush 里抠代码"，而是**理解 Crush 的架构后，用自己的方式重写核心逻辑**。核心就一个循环：

```
for {
    // 1. 调 LLM API（stream 模式）
    stream := llm.Stream(messages, tools)

    for each event in stream {
        switch event.Type {
        case Text:
            print(event.Text)
        case ToolCall:
            result := executeTool(event.ToolName, event.Args)
            messages.append(ToolResult{result})
        }
    }

    // 2. 如果 LLM 没有要求调工具，结束
    if no tool calls {
        break
    }
    // 3. 否则把 tool result 发回给 LLM，继续循环
}
```

这个循环在 Crush 里就是 `agent.Stream()` 的 `PrepareStep` + `OnToolCall` + `OnToolResult` 回调组合。**你不需要 Crush 的任何一行代码，只需要理解这个 Pattern**。

```go
// Core loop
func (a *Agent) Run(sessionID, userInput string) (*Result, error) {
    sess := a.sessions.GetOrCreate(sessionID)
    
    // Step 1: Add user message
    sess.AddMessage(RoleUser, userInput)
    
    // Step 2: Check context limits
    a.contextManager.CompressIfNeeded(sess)
    
    for turn := 0; turn < MaxTurns; turn++ {
        // Step 3: Call LLM
        response, err := a.llm.Chat(sess.Messages, a.tools.Schema())
        
        // Step 4: Parse response
        choice := response.Choices[0]
        
        if choice.Message.ToolCalls != nil {
            // Execute each tool
            for _, tc := range choice.Message.ToolCalls {
                sess.AddMessage(RoleAssistant, toolCall part)
                result := a.tools.Execute(tc.Function.Name, tc.Function.Arguments)
                sess.AddToolResult(tc.ID, result)
            }
            // Continue loop
        } else {
            // Text response - done
            sess.AddMessage(RoleAssistant, choice.Message.Content)
            return &Result{Content: choice.Message.Content}, nil
        }
    }
    
    return nil, ErrMaxTurns
}
```

{"content": "# Crush Agent — Core Agent Runtime

A self-implemented AI Agent Runtime in Go, built from scratch without any agent frameworks (LangGraph, OpenHands, etc.). Uses only an OpenAI-compatible API client as the LLM provider — the core loop, tool system, session management, and context management are all custom.

## Architecture

```
cmd/server/main.go          Entry point, HTTP server
internal/
  agent/
    agent.go                Core agent loop (LLM → Tool → LLM)
    tool.go                 Tool interface + Registry
    session.go              Session management (multi-window)
    context.go              Context window + compression
  tools/
    calculator.go           Arithmetic expression evaluator
    search.go               Mock search engine
    todo.go                 Todo list manager
  web/
    handler.go              Web UI (chat + trace viewer)
```

### Core Loop (`internal/agent/agent.go`)

```
┌──────────┐    ┌──────────┐    ┌───────────┐
│  User    │───→│  LLM     │───→│  Tool     │
│  Input   │    │  Stream  │    │  Execute  │
└──────────┘    └──────────┘    └───────────┘
                     │                │
                     │  (no tools)    │  (tools used)
                     ↓                ↓
                  Response        Loop back to LLM
                  to user         with tool results
```

Key design: The agent does NOT use any agent framework. The `LLM → parse tool calls → execute tools → feed results back → continue` cycle is entirely self-implemented in `Agent.Run()`.

### Tool Registration

Each tool implements the `Tool` interface:

```go
type Tool interface {
    Name() string
    Description() string
    Parameters() map[string]any  // JSON Schema
    Execute(ctx context.Context, params map[string]any) (string, error)
}
```

Tools are registered in a `Registry` which generates OpenAI-compatible function schemas automatically. The LLM decides which tool to call based on name + description + parameter schema.

### Session Management

- Independent sessions identified by string ID (e.g. "window-1", "window-2")
- Each session has its own message history and turn count
- Sessions don't interfere with each other — user can switch between conversations freely
- In-memory storage (no database needed)

### Context Management

- `MaxTurns`: limit on conversation turns before compression
- `MaxMessages`: limit on stored messages before trimming
- When limits are exceeded:
  1. Trim to `MaxMessages`, keeping the first message + last N-1
  2. Older messages are replaced with a system summary message
  3. Turn counter is reset to allow continued conversation

## Quick Start

### Prerequisites

- Go 1.22+
- OpenAI API key (or any OpenAI-compatible API)

### Run

```bash
# Set your API key
export OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...

# Run the web server
go run ./cmd/server/

# With custom model and API endpoint
go run ./cmd/server/ --model gpt-4o-mini --api-url https://api.openai.com/v1
```

Open http://localhost:8080 in your browser.

### Usage Examples

Try these prompts in the web UI:

1. **Calculator**: "What is 2 + 2?" or "Calculate sqrt(144) * 3"
2. **Search**: "What's the capital of France?" or "Tell me about Go language"
3. **Todo**: "Add 'buy groceries' to my todo list" then "Show my todos"
4. **Multi-session**: Open the sidebar, create a new session, ask different questions
5. **Follow-up**: "What's 15% of 200?" then "Add that result to my todo list"

## Project Structure

```
crush-agent/
├── cmd/server/main.go        # Entry point
├── internal/
│   ├── agent/                # Core runtime
│   │   ├── agent.go          # Agent loop (Run, callLLM, executeToolCall)
│   │   ├── tool.go           # Tool interface + Registry
│   │   ├── session.go        # Session Manager + Session
│   │   ├── context.go        # Context window + compression
│   │   ├── tool_test.go      # Registry tests
│   │   ├── session_test.go   # Session tests
│   │   └── context_test.go   # Context tests
│   ├── tools/                # Tool implementations
│   │   ├── calculator.go     # Arithmetic evaluator
│   │   ├── search.go         # Mock search
│   │   ├── todo.go           # Todo list
│   │   └── tools_test.go     # Tool tests
│   └── web/
│       └── handler.go        # HTTP server + embedded HTML UI
├── go.mod
└── README.md
```

## Testing

```bash
# Run all tests
go test ./...

# Run specific package tests
go test ./internal/agent/...
go test ./internal/tools/...
```

### What's Tested

| Package | Tests |
|---------|-------|
| `agent` | Tool registry (register/get/list/schemas/execute), Session CRUD, Message management, Context compression |
| `tools` | Calculator (arithmetic, scientific, edge cases), Search (exact/fuzzy/no match), Todo (add/list/done/clear) |

## System Design

### Memory Recall Strategy

The context manager uses two compression strategies:

1. **Message Trimming** (when `len(messages) > MaxMessages`): Keep the first user message (establishes conversation context) + the last N-1 messages (recent history). This preserves the conversation's original intent while discarding mid-conversation noise.

2. **Summarization** (when `turns > MaxTurns`): Build a summary of older messages by extracting: user questions, tool names used, assistant replies. Replace older messages with a `system` message containing `[Summary of earlier conversation: ...]`. This allows the LLM to maintain context without token overflow.

**When summarization triggers**: After each LLM call in the agent loop, `ContextManager.CheckAndCompress()` runs. If either threshold is exceeded, compression happens before the next LLM call.

### What's Included in Context

- **User messages**: All user inputs (essential for understanding the request)
- **Assistant messages**: LLM responses and tool calls (shows reasoning)
- **Tool results**: Output from each tool execution (needed for next LLM step)
- **Summary messages**: Compressed history when context grows too long

The guiding principle: include everything needed for the LLM to understand the conversation state, discard what can be reconstructed from recent context.

## Dependencies

Only external dependency: `github.com/sashabaranov/go-openai` (OpenAI API client — not an agent framework).

## Comparison with Crush

This is a simplified version of the core ideas in [Crush](https://github.com/charmbracelet/crush). Crush is a production-grade terminal AI assistant with ~16k lines in its agent runtime, including multi-provider support, SQLite persistence, pub/sub, MCP protocol, OAuth refresh, and a full TUI. This project extracts the **essential architecture** in ~1,800 lines of source code.
